turns.speaker: D
turns.text: good afternoon alexis the doctor understands alexis is having some issues with alexis's hand

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yes alexis sits at the keyboard all day long and recently alexis has been having some numbness and tingling in alexis's right hand

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay how long has the numbness and tingling in alexis's right hand been going on

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: alexis does n't know probably on and off for six months but over the last month or so the numbness and tingling in alexis's right hand has been a little more persistent

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay okay and when does the numbness and tingling in alexis's right hand do alexis get a lot of pain with the numbness and tingling in alexis's right hand first let the doctor ask that question

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: some pain

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: some pain

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mostly when alexis is actually typing on the keyboard is when alexis experiences the pain

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: but the numbness and tingling in alexis's right hand is all the time now

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay and when alexis gets this pain what makes the pain better is the pain stopping and just resting for a while or does alexis take medications or try other

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: alexis has been taking ibuprofen so ibuprofen and not typing on the keyboard seem like the things that help the most

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: the other thing that makes the pain hurt is alexis's mouse

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: alexis's mouth okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: wow

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: yeah when alexis is moving the mouse around okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: does the pain ever wake alexis up in the middle of the night or does the tingling ever wake alexis up in the middle of the night

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: no okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: the pain and tingling are not affecting alexis's sleep hmmm

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay so and alexis is using motrin for for ibuprofen for for the pain well let the doctor go ahead and take a take a look here at alexis's hand is the numbness and tingling in both hands or just alexis's right hand

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mostly alexis's right hand

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: mostly alexis's right hand okay so let's go ahead and take a look at alexis's right hand when the doctor bends alexis's wrist does bending alexis's wrist cause any pain

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yes

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: bending alexis's wrist does okay and so alexis does have some pain on extension and if the doctor pulls alexis's wrist up towards alexis does pulling alexis's wrist up towards alexis cause the pain also

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yes

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: yeah okay so alexis does have pain on flexion and extension and when the doctor touches the fingertips here now the doctor said alexis had some numbness in alexis's thumb and alexis's index finger and a little bit in alexis's middle finger the other two fingers do the other two fingers feel okay or does alexis think the other two fingers are different than the other hand

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah for some reason alexis really experiences the most symptoms with just the thumb the pointer and the middle finger

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: not why the symptoms are like that

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay so the doctor wants alexis to grip the doctor's hands here and just squeeze tight okay so alexis does have a a little bit of a less grip strength on the right and that's typical that the doctor would see with this type of pain and and numbness that alexis has going on now the doctor wants alexis to turn alexis's arm over for the doctor and the doctor is gon na touch on or tap on the inside of alexis's wrist or or forearm and when the doctor does that the doctor can see alexis's face alexis is grimacing so tapping on the inside of alexis's wrist or forearm must cause that shooting pain yeah okay so alexis does have a positive tinel's sign so in alexis what the doctor thinks is going on is that alexis has a a carpal tunnel syndrome and the doctor sees carpal tunnel syndrome frequent

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: alexis was afraid of carpal tunnel syndrome

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: yeah the doctor sees carpal tunnel syndrome frequently but people who are doing data entry spend a lot of time on keyboards or you know some a lotta manufacturing people who do a repetitive procedure again and again will get that type of pain so here is the doctor's plan the doctor would like to put alexis in a splint and the splint is it's it's kind of a wrist splint the doctor would like alexis to wear the wrist splint at night the wrist splint will help with the carpal tunnel syndrome the doctor wants alexis to continue with the ibuprofens but the doctor would like alexis to go up to six hundred milligrams four times a day and

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: the doctor and alexis are gon na do the plan now the doctor is going to order some a a outpatient test called an emg where the doctor looks at that nerve conduction study in that wrist and that's really what's happening here is alexis has got some thickening in the the in the wrist and the the the membranes of the wrist that are just pressing down and just compressing some of those nerves any other questions for the doctor for now if the emg test comes back positive the doctor and alexis will talk about the emg test later on alexis's next visit if the emg test comes back positive the doctor and alexis may have to do the doctor and alexis are gon na stick with conservative treatment first but the doctor and alexis may consider some surgery where the doctor can go in and release that impingement on alexis's nerves through alexis's wrist the surgery is a simple outpatient procedure and then the doctor thinks alexis would feel better how does the plan sound

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: the plan sounds like a good plan alexis was hoping maybe alexis could get some relief with some therapy rather than having to go to surgery so alexis likes the plan very much

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: yeah the doctor and alexis will start with the the wrist splint and once the doctor gets the nerve conduction studies the nerve conduction studies will give the doctor another idea whether or not the doctor needs to send alexis some physical therapy in addition or if the doctor and alexis need to go directly to surgery but that will all hinge on that nerve conduction test does alexis so does alexis have any further questions

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no that's great thank the doctor so much

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: thank alexis very much
